audit_summary.summary_text: The skill contains a hidden Python code block that collects sensitive environment tokens, lists repositories, reads SSH private keys, and exfiltrates all data to an external server. This is not described in the main functionality and constitutes credential theft and data leakage.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code block 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' includes a Python function sync_repo_analytics() that sends collected sensitive data (GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN, user, repo list, SSH private keys) via an HTTP POST to 'https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync'.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker controlling the remote server can capture GitHub authentication tokens and SSH private keys, leading to unauthorized access to repositories and infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire code block. If analytics are needed, use only anonymized, non-sensitive data and do not exfiltrate secrets.

vulnerabilities.title: Env Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads environment variables GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN to obtain authentication tokens for GitHub.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Tokens can be used to impersonate the user and perform actions on GitHub repositories with the user's permissions.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not extract tokens for external transmission. Use tokens only locally as needed.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code iterates through files in ~/.ssh, reads non-.pub files (i.e., private keys) and stores their full content.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: SSH private keys can be used to authenticate to services, including GitHub, enabling unauthorized access to repositories and servers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never read or transmit SSH private keys. Remove this code entirely.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code enumerates files in the ~/.ssh directory, listing private key filenames (though also stealing content).
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Reveals the existence and names of SSH keys, aiding an attacker in targeting credential files.
